overrides.override_summary: Fast Food Act establishes $20/hr minimum wage for fast food employees; Council may raise 3.5%/yr through 2028
overrides.why_it_matters: Directly increases labor costs for CA franchisees; may affect unit economics significantly vs. non-CA stores

overrides.override_summary: DPF provides no training or assistance for Fast Food Act compliance
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee bears full compliance burden

overrides.override_summary: Choice of forum exception for claims under Illinois law
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees can litigate IL franchise law claims in Illinois

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law exception for Illinois law
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law governs IL-law claims

overrides.override_summary: All initial fees deferred until pre-opening obligations complete; development fees deferred until first franchise opens
overrides.why_it_matters: Reduces upfront cash burden for MD franchisees; indicates MD Securities Commissioner identified financial assurance concerns about DPF

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law liability
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain all MD franchise law claims regardless of release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor acknowledges bankruptcy termination limitation

overrides.override_summary: 3-year SOL for MD Franchise Law claims from grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Time-limited window for MD claims

overrides.override_summary: Litigation cannot be required outside Minnesota; no jury trial waiver; no liquidated damages/termination penalties/judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: Comprehensive protection for MN franchisees; prevents penalty clauses

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination with 60 days to cure
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer cure period than FA default (7-30 days)

overrides.override_summary: 180 days notice for non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: 6-month advance warning for MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Consent to transfer not unreasonably withheld when transferee meets standards
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor discretion on transfers

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes Minn. Rule 2860.4400D claims
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain MN franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: $30 service charge cap
overrides.why_it_matters: Caps certain fees charged to MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in conflicts of law
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law supersedes FA terms

overrides.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA on termination/renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: State statute may override FA termination provisions

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void if employee earnings <$100K/yr; independent contractor <$250K/yr (adjusted for inflation)
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly limits post-term noncompete enforceability for most store-level employees

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring employees of other franchisees or franchisor per RCW 49.62.060
overrides.why_it_matters: No-poach provisions void in WA; supported by 2018 AOD with WA Attorney General

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes WA Franchise Act rights unless negotiated with independent counsel after agreement in effect
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees retain all WA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: Transfer fee ($1,500) must reflect actual costs
